title: erigeron corymbosus
intro: Erigeron corymbosus, also known as the flowering stemless daisy or cotton-top daisy, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its daisy-like flowers and edible leaves. The medicinal properties of Erigeron corymbosus include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe wounds, and support the immune system. Erigeron corymbosus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for medicinal use, and its leaves can be consumed raw or cooked as a nutritious addition to salads and other dishes.
